options.name: --workingdirectory
options.description:
Working directory for the
code compiler. Defaults to
current directory.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: exec
commands.description: Run a script from a DLL.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enables debug output.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: init
commands.description:
Creates a sample script along with the
launch.json file needed to launch and
debug the script.

options.name: --workingdirectory
options.description:
Working directory for
initialization. Defaults to
current directory.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description:
(Optional) The name of the
sample script file to be
created during
initialization. Defaults to
'main.csx'

options.name: --workingdirectory
options.description:
Working directory the new
script file to be created.
Defaults to current
directory.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: publish
commands.description:
Creates a self contained executable or DLL
from a script

options.name: --output
options.description:
Directory where the published executable
should be placed. Defaults to a 'publish'
folder in the current directory.

options.name: --name
options.description:
The name for the generated DLL (executable
not supported at this time). Defaults to
the name of the script.

options.name: --dll
options.description:
Publish to a .dll instead of an
executable.

options.name: --configuration
options.description:
Configuration to use for publishing the
script [Release/Debug]. Default is "Debug"

options.name: --runtime
options.description:
The runtime used when publishing the self
contained executable. Defaults to your
current runtime.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

commands.name: register
commands.description:
Register .csx file handler to enable
running scripts directly
Run ' [command] -?|-h|--help' for more information about a command.
Starting without a path to a CSX file or a command, starts the REPL (interactive) mode.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

options.name: --interactive
options.description:
Execute a script and drop into the
interactive mode afterwards.

options.name: --configuration
options.description:
Configuration to use for running the
script [Release/Debug] Default is "Debug"

options.name: --sources
options.description:
Specifies a NuGet package source to use
when resolving NuGet packages.

options.name: --cache-path
options.description:
Specify the temporary directory that the
script is built in.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enables debug output.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description:
Set the verbosity level of the command.
Allowed values are t[trace], d[ebug],
i[nfo], w[arning], e[rror], and
c[ritical].

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Disable caching (Restore and Dll cache)

options.name: --disable-isolated-load-context
options.description: Disables isolated assembly load context

options.name: --info
options.description: Displays environmental information

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information.
